MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Yang and Henry Yu for Chun Yen Chen, Administrator (via Zoom).
Robert Cross for and with Mikki May Chen, Objector (via Zoom).

Counsel reported that the case had not been resolved through mediation, and agreed further mediation was unlikely to be fruitful and requested that the matter be set for trial.

Mr. Yang estimated trial would take approximately two full days. Mr. Cross disagreed, estimating it could be completed in a singer afternoon. The Court explained that trials in Department 204 are conducted on Friday mornings from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m., and if the estimated trial time exceeds six hours total, the case must be referred to Department 206 for assignment.

Discussion was held regarding possible trial dates, with counsel indicating a preference for December 2025. Counsel also discussed the status of discovery, noting that no depositions had been taken and the issues were relatively limited. Mr. Cross stated he may file a motion for summary judgment.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to August 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and indicated it would issue a Case Management Order in advance of that date. The Court requested that counsel meet and confer to determine possible stipulations to reduce trial time. The hearing on status of mediation was taken off calendar.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Yang appeared by zoom for Chun Yen Chen (petitioner).
Robert Cross for Miki Chen (mother of the decedent/beneficiary).

The parties reported that they thought they had a settlement in principle, that there is a dispute on a fundamental issue which impacts the settlement amount and asked for the court's assistance. Further discussion was held regarding the parties having gone to mediation with Michael Freedman in October of last year, but being too far apart, the possibility of using the services of the court's ADR Department and whether it makes sense to set the matter for trial. The clerk provided the parties with the ADR Department e-mail address.
